title: Dame Jenni Murray: Former BBC Woman's Hour host dies at 75
description: She was the Radio 4 programme's longest-serving presenter, having left in 2020 after 33 years.
content:
Broadcaster Dame Jenni Murray, who hosted BBC Radio 4's Woman's Hour for more than three decades, has died at the age of 75.
Dame Jenni joined the programme in 1987 and left in 2020 as its longest-serving presenter.

title: Thousands vaccinated in meningitis outbreak in Kent as experts uncertain over peak
description: The outbreak, which has killed two people, is thought to have originated at a Canterbury nightclub.
content:
More than 4,500 young people have been vaccinated and over 10,000 treated with antibiotics in a huge drive to tackle a deadly meningitis outbreak in Kent, as health officials say it is too early to say if it has reached its peak.

title: Harry Maguire and Kobbie Mainoo recalled in 35-man England squad
description: Thomas Tuchel has named a larger than usual group, with 11 established players to be rested for the first of two pre-World Cup friendlies.
content: Thomas Tuchel has recalled Harry Maguire and Kobbie Mainoo for the Wembley friendlies against Uruguay and Japan in an enlarged England squad of 35 players. The manager said he has picked almost two groups for the matches in order to give opportunitie... [3485 chars]

title: Russia offered to curb its support for Iran if US halted aid to Ukraine
description: US rejected deal on intelligence-sharing proposed by Vladimir Putin’s envoy last week
content:
The US rejected an offer from Russia this month to stop sharing targeting data with Iran if Washington agreed to end its intelligence partnership with Ukraine, according to two people familiar with the matter.

title: Russia Offered to End Iran Intelligence Sharing if U.S. Halted Ukraine Support - Politico
description: Russia offered to stop its intelligence-sharing with Iran in exchange for the United States suspending intelligence support to Ukraine, but Washington rejected the offer, Politico reported Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.
content:
Russia offered to stop its intelligence-sharing with Iran in exchange for the United States suspending intelligence support to Ukraine, but Washington rejected the offer, Politico reported Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.
